requirements.summary: Create a workout entry form that allows users to input exercise name, sets, reps, weight, duration, and optional notes
acceptanceCriteria: Form includes all required input fields
Users receive confirmation after successful workout submission

file_operations.description: Create a workout entry form component with input fields for exercise name, sets, reps, weight, duration, and notes. Use the authorization component to ensure only authenticated users can submit workouts. Implement form validation for required fields and numeric constraints. On successful submission, call the backend's addWorkout method and display a success message. Include the dumbbell icon from frontend/public/assets/generated/dumbbell-icon.dim_128x128.png as a visual element in the form header. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a custom hook using React Query's useMutation to handle workout submission. The hook should call actor.addWorkout with the workout session data, invalidate the workout history query cache on success, and provide loading/error states to the form component.

Create a page component that renders the WorkoutEntryForm component. Include proper layout, authentication guards using the authorization component to ensure only logged-in users can access this page, and navigation elements.

Create a login/logout button component following the authorization component's usage instructions. Use the useInternetIdentity hook to manage login state. On logout, clear the React Query cache to remove all cached application data. Display the login button in the Layout component header. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a modal component that prompts new users to enter their name on first login. Use the getCallerUserProfile backend method to check if a profile exists. If null, show the modal. On submission, call saveCallerUserProfile with the entered name. Implement proper loading states to prevent modal flash following the authorization component's pattern for useGetCallerUserProfile. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a custom hook using React Query's useQuery to fetch the current user's profile by calling actor.getCallerUserProfile. Follow the authorization component's pattern to prevent profile setup modal flash by properly handling actor loading states and returning custom isLoading and isFetched flags. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create a custom hook using React Query's useMutation to save the current user's profile by calling actor.saveCallerUserProfile. Invalidate the currentUserProfile query cache on success.

requirements.summary: Build a workout history view that displays all logged workouts in reverse chronological order with date, exercise, and key metrics
acceptanceCriteria: All workout sessions are displayed in a clear, organized list
Key metrics (sets, reps, weight) are visible at a glance

file_operations.description: Create a component that displays workout history in reverse chronological order. Fetch workout data using the getOwnWorkoutHistory backend method through a custom hook. Display each workout session with date, exercise name, sets, reps, weight, duration, and notes in a card or list format. Show loading and empty states appropriately.

Create a custom hook using React Query's useQuery to fetch the current user's workout history by calling actor.getOwnWorkoutHistory. Enable the query only when the actor is available and the user is authenticated. Sort the results in reverse chronological order by date.

Create a page component that renders the WorkoutHistoryList component. Include authentication guards using the authorization component to ensure only logged-in users can view their workout history. Add proper layout and navigation elements.

requirements.summary: Implement a progression visualization that shows performance trends over time for each exercise, displaying improvements in weight, reps, or volume
acceptanceCriteria: Users can view progression charts for individual exercises
Charts display trends over time for key metrics
Visual representation clearly shows improvement or changes in performance

file_operations.description: Create a chart component using a lightweight charting library (e.g., recharts) to visualize workout progression. Accept workout sessions filtered by exercise name as props. Display line or bar charts showing trends over time for weight, reps, sets, and calculated volume (sets × reps × weight). Include axis labels, tooltips, and a legend for clarity.

Create a dropdown or button group component that allows users to select an exercise from their workout history. Extract unique exercise names from the workout data and provide a selection interface to filter the progression chart.

Create a page component that combines the ExerciseSelector and ProgressionChart components. Use the workout history data from useWorkoutHistory hook, filter it based on the selected exercise, and pass the filtered data to the chart. Include authentication guards using the authorization component to ensure only logged-in users can view progression analytics.
Add recharts as a dependency for charting functionality (e.g., "recharts": "^2.10.0").

requirements.summary: Design a creative and coherent visual theme with a fitness-inspired aesthetic, using energetic colors, bold typography, and motivational design elements that remain consistent throughout the application
acceptanceCriteria: Visual theme is applied consistently across all pages and components
Color palette and typography choices support readability and user engagement
Design aesthetic aligns with fitness and performance tracking context

file_operations.description: Update global styles with a fitness-inspired color palette featuring energetic colors (e.g., vibrant oranges, blues, or greens for accents). Apply bold typography for headings and motivational text. Ensure consistent spacing, border-radius, and shadow styles throughout. Use the hero-fitness background image from frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-fitness.dim_1200x600.png as a hero section background or page header element.

Extend the Tailwind theme with custom fitness-inspired colors (e.g., primary, accent, success colors), custom font families for bold typography, and custom spacing/border-radius values that align with the energetic design aesthetic. Define shadow and animation utilities to support motivational design elements.

Create a layout component that wraps all pages with consistent header, navigation, and footer elements. Include the hero-fitness background image from frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-fitness.dim_1200x600.png in the header or hero section. Apply the fitness-inspired theme colors and typography. Include a navigation bar with links to workout entry, history, and progression pages. Integrate the LoginButton component from the authorization component for user authentication. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wrap the application with the Layout component. Integrate the ProfileSetupModal to show on first login when the user has no profile. Register routes for AddWorkoutPage, WorkoutHistoryPage, and ProgressionPage using React Router. Set WorkoutHistoryPage as the default route for authenticated users. Ensure all routes are protected with authentication guards using the authorization component. Add route transitions and error boundaries as needed. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.
